title: Performance-Aware Error Handling
description: Apply performance-conscious error handling: use EAFP pattern for dict access, batch-process items collecting errors, measure error rates, and implement conditional retry logic.
starterCode:

def conditional_retry(func, should_retry_func, max_attempts=3):

    Return result on success, raise last exception on failure."""

hints: EAFP: 'Easier to Ask Forgiveness than Permission' — use try/except instead of checking first
Catch both KeyError and TypeError in lbyl_vs_eafp_key for robustness
In batch_process, continue processing remaining items after an error
Store the index and item in error dicts for debugging context
error_rate divides error count by total — handle empty input list
conditional_retry should only retry when should_retry_func returns True

testCases.description: Test EAFP key access with existing key
Test EAFP key access with missing key returns default
Test batch_process with all successful items
